arts.ttl: Newcastle owners PIF 'close' to stunning £800m investment as exciting prediction made
arts.sum: Newcastle United's majority owners, the Saudi Arabian Public Investment Fund (PIF), are reportedly nearing a $1billion (around £818m) acquisition of a minority stake in sport streaming platform DAZN. arts.ttl: Newcastle United prepare to say goodbye to Miguel Almiron after MLS approach
arts.sum: Newcastle United have received a new offer for Miguel Almiron - and the Magpies won't stand in his way if he wants to leave this month. United have been approached by Almiron's old club Atlanta United of Major League Soccer.

arts.ttl: Fabian Schar's Newcastle United absence explained as Eddie Howe rejigs defence vs Wolves
arts.sum: With Sven Botman now fit again his chances of Premier League action will be reduced further going into 2025. The team sheet showed that Sven Botman and Dan Burn were the preferred centre-back pairing for Eddie Howe against the men from the Black Country.

arts.ttl: Everton v Aston Villa, Newcastle v Wolves and more: Premier League – live
arts.sum: Updates from Wednesday evening’s matches Live scoreboard | Premier League top scorers | Email Niall Leicester manager Ruud van Nistelrooy is hoping the 6-2 FA Cup rout of QPR will boost morale: “We’ve been talking about recent results, and breaking the cycle. We were able to rest some players, taking them off early because of the score. It’s important to start climbing, start collecting points. Our performances have been decent, but individual mistakes have cost us.” Newcastle (4-3-3): Dubravka; Livramento, Botman, Burn, Hall; Guimarães, Tonali, Joelinton; Jacob Murphy, Isak, Gordon. Subs: Vlachodimos, Trippier, Krafth, Osula, Almirón, Kelly, Willock, Longstaff, Miley. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Giants clash, Brazilian showdown - battles that could decide Newcastle vs Wolves
arts.sum: Burn has won the third-most headers of any Premier League player this season, while Strand Larsen ranks just 16th. As with every other outfield Premier League opponent, Burn will look down on Wolves striker Strand Larsen.
